title: CKC ClaimGraph
description: The serialized Conventional Knowledge Commits ClaimGraph: claims (nodes) and their relation edges, with computed effective status and in-question flags.

timeline.description: Optional per-commit replay frames (the time dimension): the graph state after each knowledge commit, oldest first. Present only when built with `claimgraph build --timeline`. The nodes/edges above remain the final state.

id.description: Stable identifier: a Lean FQN, a blueprint label, or a registry slug.

kind.description: Claim kind or the asserting commit type (conjecture, finding, definition, ...).

status.description: Asserted status from the latest commit that set one.

effective_status.description: Minimum status over the node and its transitive Depends-On/Assumes closure.

in_question.description: True when a dependency is broken (disproved/falsified/not-replicated).

weakest_dep.description: The dependency whose status set the effective status, if weaker than self.

lean.description: Lean fully-qualified declaration name(s) for this claim (blueprint integration).

aliases.description: Other ids (label / FQN / slug) this node was unified from during reconciliation.

claimed.description: Blueprint reading: machine-checked iff the node carries \leanok / \mathlibok.

asserted.description: Commit-history reading: the latest commit Status: for this claim.

kernel.description: Kernel reading from #print axioms (via axiom-report): machine-checked/axiomatised/open.

agreement.description: Cross-history reconciliation category. kernel-refutes-claim is a validity gap; blueprint-incomplete is a coverage gap (machine-checked but uses an unformalized concept).

blueprint_complete.description: Blueprint coverage: true iff every concept this claim \uses is modelled in Lean. null when coverage does not apply (a prose-only node). Orthogonal to validity.

uses_gap.description: An example node in the \uses closure that has no Lean formalization, when blueprint_complete is false.

frame.description: One replay frame: the graph state immediately after a commit.

hash.description: Commit hash (%H) for git histories; null for fixtures.

date.description: ISO-8601 author date (%aI); null for fixtures (axis is commit ordinal).

event.description: The salient event this commit performed, for the replay highlight.

focus.description: The focal claim id (the refuted / promoted / asserted node).

present.description: Node ids that exist as of this commit.

edges.description: Edge ids ('source|relation|target') present as of this commit.

state.description: Per-present-node snapshot keyed by node id.
